seoTitle: How to Download Google Docs (2026 Complete Guide)
metaDescription: Download a Google Doc as Word, PDF, or plain text using the File menu, plus how to save offline copies from the mobile app on Android or iOS.
intro: Sometimes you need a document off the cloud entirely, whether for backup, sharing with someone who doesn't use Google Docs, or archiving. This guide covers exporting your file through the Download menu in several formats, including Word and PDF, and shows how to do the same from the mobile app when you're away from a computer.

steps.heading: Open the File menu
steps.body: With your document open, click File in the top menu to access the download and export options.

steps.heading: Hover over Download
steps.body: Select Download to open the extended list of available file formats for exporting your document.

steps.heading: Choose your target format
steps.body: Pick Microsoft Word (.docx) or PDF Document (.pdf) for the most common use cases, and the download will begin immediately.

steps.heading: Consider alternative formats
steps.body: For specific needs, you can also choose Rich Text Format (.rtf), Plain Text (.txt), or a zipped Web Page (.html) export.

steps.heading: Download from mobile
steps.body: In the Google Docs app on Android or iOS, use the file options menu to save an offline copy directly to your device.

steps.heading: Organize your downloaded files
steps.body: Keep track of your destination folder and file names so exported documents stay easy to find later.

faq.question: Which format keeps my formatting most intact when I download?
faq.answer: PDF generally preserves layout and formatting most reliably, since it locks in exact spacing and fonts regardless of the device opening it.

faq.question: Can I edit a document after downloading it as a Word file?
faq.answer: Yes, a downloaded .docx file opens in Microsoft Word or compatible editors and can be edited independently from the original Google Doc.

faq.question: Do I need an internet connection to download a document?
faq.answer: You need a connection to initiate the download from Google Docs, but once saved locally, the file is available offline.

faq.question: Is downloading available on the mobile app?
faq.answer: Yes, the Google Docs mobile app lets you save offline copies directly to your phone's storage.
